items.title: UEFA Champions League
items.summary: Newcastle head to Azerbaijan to take on Qarabag in the Champions League play-offs.

items.title: Gordon channels Shearer after breaking his record
items.summary: Newcastle's Anthony Gordon only had one thought in his mind as his side faced Qarabag in the Champions League...

items.title: Woede en ongeloof bij Arsenal: 'Ze geven de titelrace uit handen'
items.summary: Arsenal had een geweldige voorsprong opgebouwd in de Premier League, maar heeft woensdagavond wederom punten laten liggen. In de 94ste minuut kwam Wolverhampton Wanderers langszij: 2-2. Daardoor was de verslagenheid groot.

items.title: Inter worstelt in Noorse vrieskou: 'Italiaanse nachtmerrie staat voor de deur'
items.summary: Internazionale ging woensdagavond flink onderuit bij FK Bodø/Glimt . De Italiaanse media zagen de ploeg van Cristian Chivu worstelen in de Noorse vrieskou.

items.title: Arsenal squander two-goal lead as Wolves score late to earn draw
items.summary: A Ricardo Calafiori own goal in the 94th minute gives Wolves a point. as the Premier League's bottom side come back from two goals down against leaders Arsenal at Molineux.

items.title: Arsenal suffer title blow with dramatic Wolves draw
items.summary: Debutant Tom Edozie scored a dramatic last-gasp equaliser as Wolves came from two goals down to snatch a 2-2 draw with Arsenal and plunge the Gunners' Premier League title hopes into further doubt.

items.title: Vinícius Jr. racism allegation prompts UEFA probe
items.summary: UEFA will investigate the racism allegations made by Vinícius Júnior in Real Madrid's game against Benfica.

items.title: Gordon makes UCL history with 4 goals in 1st half
items.summary: Newcastle United forward Anthony Gordon has become just the second player to score four goals in the first half of a UEFA Champions League game and the first to do so in the knockout stage.

items.title: Bodo/Glimt add win over Inter to impressive run
items.summary: Champions League runner-up Inter Milan became the latest top team to suffer an upset by Bodø/Glimt in the Champions League, losing 3-1 away in the first leg of their playoff on Wednesday.

items.title: Arsenal drop MORE crucial points in title race as Wolves snatch 90+4’ equaliser!
items.summary: FREE TO WATCH: Highlights from Wolves’ match against Arsenal in the Premier League.

items.title: Arsenal suffer new blow in title race after Edozie’s equaliser boosts Wolves
items.summary: Whichever way you look at it, Arsenal did not produce a performance worthy of champions. No one in red and white will want to remember this freezing cold night at Molineux but it will live long in the memory of Wolves’ Tom Edozie, whose debut goal was a just punishment for the Premier League leaders’ ineptitude. Arsenal are five points clear of Manchester City, having played a game more, and will feel their icy breath on their shoulders after winning twice in their past seven matches. Continue reading...

items.title: European soccer recap: Bodø/Glimt stun Inter, Wolves steal point from Arsenal
items.summary: Enjoy all that happened from today's knockout phase playoff first legs, with Inter and Atléti in action. Also, Wolves took on Arsenal in the Premier League.

items.title: Timber en De Lange kennen nieuwe trainer: Beye volgt De Zerbi op bij Marseille
items.summary: Olympique Marseille heeft een week na het ontslag van Roberto De Zerbi , een akkoord bereikt met een nieuwe trainer. De Senegalees Habib Beye moet iets aan de wisselvallige resultaten van de ploeg van Quinten Timber en Jeffrey De Lange doen.

items.title: Arsenal set up Chelsea showdown in Women’s Champions League with win over Leuven
items.summary: A hard-fought 3-1 victory over OH Leuven at Meadow Park sent Arsenal through to the quarter-finals of the Champions League. The visitors tested Renée Slegers’ side when Sara Pusztai cancelled out Alessia Russo’s goal but a penalty from Mariona Caldentey and second from Russo secured the win, earning them a comfortable 7-1 aggregate score. Confidence is flying high around Arsenal, they are almost at the end of one of the most taxing periods of the season to date. Since the return from the winter break, they have won seven of their nine fixtures and lost just one, featured in five different competitions, lifted the inaugural Champions Cup and now reached the quarter-finals of the Women’s Champions League. It has been a month that will have tested every single one of the playing squad and staff to their limits but one that will have given them belief that they can still challenge on multiple fronts. Continue reading...

items.title: Poku belangrijk voor Leverkusen met assist tijdens Griekse herhaling
items.summary: Bayer Leverkusen heeft woensdagavond een knappe overwinning geboekt in de tussenronde van de Champions League. Ernest Poku was belangrijk voor de Duitsers bij de 0-2 zege op Olympiakos.

items.title: Watch all four Gordon goals against Qarabag
items.summary: Watch Anthony Gordon's four goals against Qarabag, making him Newcastle's top scorer in the Champions League (10), overtaking Alan Shearer (6).

items.title: Late DRAMA! Edozie smashes in 90+4' equaliser against Arsenal!
items.summary: Edozie smashes in 90+4' equaliser against Arsenal in the Premier League.

items.title: Hekkensluiter Wolves laat Man City juichen: Arsenal geeft het in blessuretijd weg
items.summary: Arsenal heeft woensdag twee punten laten liggen tegen Wolverhampton Wanderers. De ploeg van Mikel Arteta leek lang op weg naar de zege, maar in de blessuretijd pakte de hekkensluiter een punt. Dat betekent het tweede puntenverlies op rij voor de koploper van Engeland.

items.title: Game on?! Bueno curls in sensational strike to halve Wolves' deficit against Arsenal
items.summary: Bueno curls in sensational strike to halve Wolves' deficit against Arsenal in the Premier League.

items.title: Anthony Gordon grabs four as Newcastle hit Qarabag for six in playoff
items.summary: When the Qarabag manager, Gurban Gurbanov, declared before kick-off that Newcastle possess “a style of play that does not suit us”, there were suggestions that he was playing mind games. Long before half-time it was fully apparent that he had rather understated things. Had this been a boxing match it would surely have been stopped by the 20th minute. Qarabag were utterly overwhelmed by the pace of their guests, and that of Anthony Gordon in particular. Gordon scored four times, boosting his tally in the Champions League this season to 10 goals. Continue reading...
